questions.question_text: Which of the following is a physical characteristic of real property that makes it unique from all other assets?
questions.explanation: Immobility is the defining physical characteristic of real property. Land is fixed in location — it cannot be relocated. This is why real estate markets are inherently local. All of the other options (scarcity, indestructibility, and uniqueness/nonhomogeneity) are also characteristics of real property, but immobility is the most fundamental physical distinction between real estate and all other asset classes.

questions.question_text: The physical characteristic of real property known as 'indestructibility' means that:
questions.correct_answer: Land cannot be destroyed, even if improvements on it are damaged
distractors: Real property values never permanently decline over any time period
A recorded deed remains valid forever and cannot be challenged
Buildings and structures cannot legally be demolished once built
questions.explanation: Indestructibility refers specifically to the land itself — the underlying earth cannot be destroyed. Improvements like buildings, fences, and landscaping can certainly be damaged, demolished, or removed. But the parcel of land remains. This characteristic contributes to land's long-term value as a durable asset, unlike most personal property which depreciates and can be destroyed.

questions.question_text: The concept that no two parcels of real property are exactly alike is called:
questions.explanation: Nonhomogeneity (also called uniqueness or heterogeneity) means that every parcel of land is different from every other parcel — even two side-by-side lots in the same subdivision differ in location, orientation, soil composition, and other attributes. This characteristic is why real estate cannot be traded on a commodities exchange like stocks or grain, and why appraisers must make adjustments when comparing properties.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an ECONOMIC characteristic of real property?
questions.explanation: Situs (area preference) is an economic characteristic — it reflects the human preference for certain locations over others, which directly drives value. The economic characteristics of real property are: scarcity (finite supply), improvements (changes to land affect value of surrounding parcels), permanence of investment (improvements are long-term), and area preference/situs. The other three options are physical characteristics.

questions.question_text: A developer builds a large apartment complex in a quiet residential neighborhood. Nearby single-family homeowners are concerned about how this will affect their property values. This concern relates to which economic characteristic of real property?
questions.explanation: The economic characteristic of 'improvements' recognizes that changes to one parcel — whether positive or negative — affect the value and desirability of neighboring properties. This is why zoning laws exist and why buyers care about neighborhood composition. A large apartment complex might increase traffic, noise, and density in ways that reduce adjacent single-family home values. Conversely, a new park or community amenity can enhance surrounding values.

questions.question_text: The economic characteristic of 'scarcity' as it applies to real property means:
questions.correct_answer: The supply of land is finite and cannot be manufactured
distractors: All parcels are unique and no two are exactly the same
Desirable locations command premium prices based on buyer preference
Real estate investments are long-term because improvements are costly
questions.explanation: Scarcity is an economic characteristic reflecting that land in any specific location cannot be created — the total supply is fixed. While you can build vertically or reclaim land at the margins, the supply of land in any particular area is limited. This contributes to upward price pressure in desirable markets. Note: scarcity alone does not create value; demand must also exist (recall the DUST elements of value).

questions.question_text: A prospective buyer is deciding between two similar homes — one on a busy arterial road and one on a quiet cul-de-sac, both priced the same. The buyer chooses the cul-de-sac home. Which characteristic of real property BEST explains this preference?
questions.explanation: Situs (area preference) is the economic characteristic that explains why location is the dominant driver of real estate value. Two structurally identical homes command different prices based solely on their location. The buyer's preference for the quieter street reflects situs in action. This is why real estate professionals consistently emphasize 'location, location, location' — it captures the economic reality that buyers pay premiums for preferred locations.

questions.question_text: An appraiser is comparing two similar office buildings. Building A is near a major highway interchange with excellent accessibility. Building B is in a less accessible location. The appraiser will likely assign a higher value to Building A primarily because of which characteristic?
questions.explanation: While scarcity may play a minor supporting role (fewer highway-adjacent properties), the primary explanation is situs — the human preference for accessible, well-located commercial space. Situs reflects that value is not just about the physical property but about the economic advantages of its specific location: customer access, employee commutes, supply chain logistics. Appraisers quantify this preference through location adjustments in the sales comparison approach.

questions.question_text: A city announces plans to build a new light rail station two blocks from a residential neighborhood. Based on the economic characteristics of real property, which outcome is MOST likely?
questions.correct_answer: Values will likely increase because the improvement enhances the area's situs
distractors: Values will decrease because land near transit is impacted by noise
Values will remain unchanged because the land's physical characteristics are unaffected
Only commercial values will increase; residential values will be unaffected
questions.explanation: Transit access improves a location's situs (area preference) for many buyers and renters, typically increasing demand and values near transit stations. This also illustrates the 'improvements' economic characteristic — a public improvement (transit infrastructure) affects the value of nearby private parcels. Research consistently shows a 'transit premium' for properties within walking distance of rail stations, though individual results depend on market conditions and specific location factors.

questions.question_text: A tornado destroys every structure on a 5-acre parcel. An insurance adjuster assesses the damage. The physical characteristic of real property that explains why the land itself retains value is:
questions.explanation: Indestructibility means the physical land cannot be destroyed. Buildings, landscaping, and other improvements can be demolished by storms, fire, or human action, but the underlying land endures. This is why land is considered a permanent asset and why lenders feel secure using it as collateral. Immobility is also true but doesn't explain the retention of value — indestructibility does.

questions.question_text: A court orders specific performance for a real estate purchase contract rather than monetary damages. This remedy is justified primarily because of which physical characteristic of real property?
questions.explanation: Specific performance — a court compelling the seller to complete the sale — is available in real estate because every parcel is unique (nonhomogeneity). Since no substitute property is identical, monetary damages alone cannot make the buyer whole. This legal principle directly stems from the physical characteristic of uniqueness. Courts generally do not grant specific performance for commodity goods that can be easily replaced.

questions.question_text: Which of the following BEST illustrates the economic characteristic of 'permanence of investment'?
questions.correct_answer: A 30-year-old building continues to generate rental income
distractors: A vacant lot appreciates due to a nearby highway project
A homeowner sells for more than the original purchase price
An investor buys farmland because the supply of land is limited
questions.explanation: Permanence of investment means that capital invested in real estate improvements tends to be long-term and relatively fixed. A building constructed decades ago still generates income — the investment is durable. A vacant lot appreciating illustrates situs or improvements. Selling for a profit illustrates appreciation (not permanence specifically). Buying due to limited supply illustrates scarcity.

questions.question_text: An investor compares real estate to stocks. She notes that she can sell stocks instantly online but cannot quickly sell a commercial property. This difference is BEST explained by which economic characteristic of real property?
questions.correct_answer: Immobility, which creates illiquidity in local markets
distractors: Scarcity, because fewer real estate investments exist than stocks
Permanence of investment, because transactions require more documentation
Situs, because location desirability makes sellers reluctant to sell
questions.explanation: Real estate's immobility means it is tied to a specific location, creating inherently local markets with fewer buyers. Unlike stocks traded on a global exchange, each property must find a buyer in its specific market. This makes real estate illiquid compared to securities. Permanence of investment contributes (improvements are fixed and long-term), but the root cause of illiquidity is that property cannot be moved to where demand is highest.

questions.question_text: The real estate saying 'location, location, location' reflects which characteristic of real property?
questions.explanation: Situs, also called area preference, is the economic characteristic that captures why location is the primary driver of real estate value. Buyers prefer certain locations for convenience, amenities, safety, schools, and aesthetics. While immobility means property is fixed in place and nonhomogeneity means each parcel is unique, situs specifically addresses the human preference that makes some locations more valuable than others.

questions.question_text: Two identical houses are built from the same floor plan. House A is in a neighborhood with top-rated schools, while House B is in an area with lower-rated schools. House A sells for $75,000 more. This price difference is BEST attributed to:
questions.correct_answer: Situs, which benefits House A through proximity to desirable schools
distractors: Scarcity of homes in the neighborhood near good schools
Nonhomogeneity making direct comparison of the parcels impossible
Permanence of investment creating different returns in each neighborhood
questions.explanation: Situs (area preference) explains why identical structures sell at vastly different prices based on location. Proximity to top-rated schools is one of the strongest situs factors affecting residential values. While nonhomogeneity confirms the parcels are technically different, the price gap is explained by buyer preference for the school district — a classic example of situs driving value. This is why real estate professionals emphasize that you cannot change a property's location.
